seoTitle: New Residence vs New Town — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between New Residence and New Town. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: New Residence vs New Town: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in New Residence and New Town.
heading: New Residence vs New Town — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between New Residence, South Australia and New Town, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: New Residence has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to New Town (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is New Residence or New Town safer from flooding?
faq.answer: New Residence has a flood risk score of 27/100, while New Town scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: New Residence scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. New Town scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in New Residence or New Town?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. New Residence has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
